verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचइति सर्वे सुसंरब्धा वाहानारुह्य दंशिता: ।स्वै: स्वैर्बलै: परिक्रान्ता अन्वीयुर्धृतकार्मुका: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaiti sarve su-saṁrabdhāvāhān āruhya daṁśitāḥsvaiḥ svair balaiḥ parikrāntāanvīyur dhṛta-kārmukāḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Having thus spoken, all those infuriated kings donned their armor and mounted their conveyances. Each king, bow in hand, was surrounded by his own army as he went after Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: तानापतत आलोक्य यादवानीकयूथपा: ।तस्थुस्तत्सम्मुखा राजन्विस्फूर्ज्य स्वधनूंषि ते ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: tān āpatata ālokyayādavānīka-yūthapāḥtasthus tat-sammukhā rājanvisphūrjya sva-dhanūṁṣi te
verses.text: The commanders of the Yādava army, seeing the enemy racing to attack, turned to face them and stood firm, O King, twanging their bows.

verses.sanskrit: अश्वपृष्ठे गजस्कन्धे रथोपस्थेऽस्‍त्र कोविदा: ।मुमुचु: शरवर्षाणि मेघा अद्रिष्वपो यथा ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: aśva-pṛṣṭhe gaja-skandherathopasthe ’stra kovidāḥmumucuḥ śara-varṣāṇimeghā adriṣv apo yathā
verses.text: Mounted on the backs of horses, the shoulders of elephants and the seats of chariots, the enemy kings, expert with weapons, rained down arrows upon the Yadus like clouds pouring rain on mountains.

verses.sanskrit: पत्युर्बलं शरासारैश्छन्नं वीक्ष्य सुमध्यमा ।सव्रीड्‍मैक्षत्तद्वक्त्रं भयविह्वललोचना ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: patyur balaṁ śarāsāraiśchannaṁ vīkṣya su-madhyamāsa-vrīḍm aikṣat tad-vaktraṁbhaya-vihvala-locanā
verses.text: Slender-waisted Rukmiṇī, seeing her Lord’s army covered by torrents of arrows, shyly looked at His face with fear-stricken eyes.

verses.sanskrit: प्रहस्य भगवानाह मा स्म भैर्वामलोचने ।विनङ्‍क्ष्यत्यधुनैवैतत्तावकै: शात्रवं बलम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: prahasya bhagavān āhamā sma bhair vāma-locanevinaṅkṣyaty adhunaivaitattāvakaiḥ śātravaṁ balam
verses.text: In response the Lord laughed and assured her, “Do not be afraid, beautiful-eyed one. This enemy force is about to be destroyed by your soldiers.”

verses.sanskrit: तेषां तद्विक्रमं वीरा गदसङ्कर्षणादय: ।अमृष्यमाणा नाराचैर्जघ्नुर्हयगजान् रथान् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: teṣāṁ tad-vikramaṁ vīrāgada-saṅkarṣanādayaḥamṛṣyamāṇā nārācairjaghnur haya-gajān rathān
verses.text: The heroes of the Lord’s army, headed by Gada and Saṅkarṣaṇa, could not tolerate the aggression of the opposing kings. Thus with iron arrows they began to strike down the enemy’s horses, elephants and chariots.

verses.sanskrit: पेतु: शिरांसि रथिनामश्विनां गजिनां भुवि ।सकुण्डलकिरीटानि सोष्णीषाणि च कोटिश: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: petuḥ śirāṁsi rathināmaśvināṁ gajināṁ bhuvisa-kuṇḍala-kirīṭānisoṣṇīṣāṇi ca koṭiśaḥ
verses.text: The heads of soldiers fighting on chariots, horses and elephants fell to the ground by the millions; some heads wore earrings and helmets, others turbans.

verses.sanskrit: हस्ता: सासिगदेष्वासा: करभा ऊरवोऽङ्‍‍घ्रय: ।अश्वाश्वतरनागोष्ट्रखरमर्त्यशिरांसि च ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: hastāḥ sāsi-gadeṣv-āsāḥkarabhā ūravo ’ṅghrayaḥaśvāśvatara-nāgoṣṭra-khara-martya-śirāṁsi ca
verses.text: Lying all around were thighs, legs and fingerless hands, along with hands clutching swords, clubs and bows, and also the heads of horses, donkeys, elephants, camels, wild asses and humans.

verses.sanskrit: हन्यमानबलानीका वृष्णिभिर्जयकाङ्‍‍क्षिभि: ।राजानो विमुखा जग्मुर्जरासन्धपुर:सरा: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: hanyamāna-balānīkāvṛṣṇibhir jaya-kāṅkṣibhiḥrājāno vimukhā jagmurjarāsandha-puraḥ-sarāḥ
verses.text: Seeing their armies being struck down by the Vṛṣṇis, who were eager for victory, the kings headed by Jarāsandha were discouraged and left the battlefield.

verses.sanskrit: शिशुपालं समभ्येत्य हृतदारमिवातुरम् ।नष्टत्विषं गतोत्साहं शुष्यद्वदनमब्रुवन् ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: śiśupālaṁ samabhyetyahṛta-dāram ivāturamnaṣṭa-tviṣaṁ gatotsāhaṁśuṣyad-vadanam abruvan
verses.text: The kings approached Śiśupāla, who was disturbed like a man who has lost his wife. His complexion was drained of color, his enthusiasm was gone, and his face appeared dried up. The kings spoke to him as follows.

verses.sanskrit: भो भो: पुरुषशार्दूल दौर्मनस्यमिदं त्यज ।न प्रियाप्रिययो राजन् निष्ठा देहिषु द‍ृश्यते ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: bho bhoḥ puruṣa-śārdūladaurmanasyam idaṁ tyajana priyāpriyayo rājanniṣṭhā dehiṣu dṛśyate
verses.text: [Jarāsandha said:] Listen, Śiśupāla, O tiger among men, give up your depression. After all, embodied beings’ happiness and unhappiness is never seen to be permanent, O King.

verses.sanskrit: यथा दारुमयी योषित् नृत्यते कुहकेच्छया ।एवमीश्वरतन्त्रोऽयमीहते सुखदु:खयो: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: yathā dāru-mayī yoṣitnṛtyate kuhakecchayāevam īśvara-tantro ’yamīhate sukha-duḥkhayoḥ
verses.text: Just as a puppet in the form of a woman dances by the desire of the puppeteer, so this world, controlled by the Supreme Lord, struggles in both happiness and misery.

verses.sanskrit: शौरे: सप्तदशाहं वै संयुगानि पराजित: ।त्रयोविंशतिभि: सैन्यैर्जिग्ये एकमहं परम् ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śaureḥ sapta-daśāhaṁ vaisaṁyugāni parājitaḥtrayo-viṁśatibhiḥ sainyairjigye ekam ahaṁ param
verses.text: In battle with Kṛṣṇa I and my twenty-three armies lost seventeen times; only once did I defeat Him.

verses.sanskrit: तथाप्यहं न शोचामि न प्रहृष्यामि कर्हिचित् ।कालेन दैवयुक्तेन जानन् विद्रावितं जगत् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathāpy ahaṁ na śocāmina prahṛṣyāmi karhicitkālena daiva-yuktenajānan vidrāvitaṁ jagat
verses.text: But still I never lament or rejoice, because I know this world is driven by time and fate.

verses.sanskrit: अधुनापि वयं सर्वे वीरयूथपयूथपा: ।पराजिता: फल्गुतन्त्रैर्यदुभि: कृष्णपालितै: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: adhunāpi vayaṁ sarvevīra-yūthapa-yūthapāḥparājitāḥ phalgu-tantrairyadubhiḥ kṛṣṇa-pālitaiḥ
verses.text: And now all of us, great commanders of military leaders, have been defeated by the Yadus and their small entourage, who are protected by Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: रिपवो जिग्युरधुना काल आत्मानुसारिणि ।तदा वयं विजेष्यामो यदा काल: प्रदक्षिण: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: ripavo jigyur adhunākāla ātmānusāriṇitadā vayaṁ vijeṣyāmoyadā kālaḥ pradakṣiṇaḥ
verses.text: Now our enemies have conquered because time favors them, but in the future, when time is auspicious for us, we shall conquer.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचएवं प्रबोधितो मित्रैश्चैद्योऽगात् सानुग: पुरम् ।हतशेषा: पुनस्तेऽपि ययु: स्वं स्वं पुरं नृपा: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaevaṁ prabodhito mitraiścaidyo ’gāt sānugaḥ puramhata-śeṣāḥ punas te ’piyayuḥ svaṁ svaṁ puraṁ nṛpāḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Thus persuaded by his friends, Śiśupāla took his followers and went back to his capital. The surviving warriors also returned to their respective cities.

verses.sanskrit: रुक्‍मी तु राक्षसोद्वाहं कृष्णद्विडसहन् स्वसु: ।पृष्ठतोऽन्वगमत् कृष्णमक्षौहिण्या वृतो बली ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: rukmī tu rākṣasodvāhaṁkṛṣṇa-dviḍ asahan svasuḥpṛṣṭhato ’nvagamat kṛṣṇamakṣauhiṇyā vṛto balī
verses.text: Powerful Rukmī, however, was especially envious of Kṛṣṇa. He could not bear the fact that Kṛṣṇa had carried off his sister to marry her in the Rākṣasa style. Thus he pursued the Lord with an entire military division.

verses.sanskrit: रुक्‍म्यमर्षी सुसंरब्ध: श‍ृण्वतां सर्वभूभुजाम् ।प्रतिजज्ञे महाबाहुर्दंशित: सशरासन: ॥ १९ ॥अहत्वा समरे कृष्णमप्रत्यूह्य च रुक्‍मिणीम् ।कुण्डिनं न प्रवेक्ष्यामि सत्यमेतद् ब्रवीमि व: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: rukmy amarṣī su-saṁrabdhaḥśṛṇvatāṁ sarva-bhūbhujāmpratijajñe mahā-bāhurdaṁśitaḥ sa-śarāsanaḥ
verses.text: Frustrated and enraged, mighty-armed Rukmī, dressed in armor and wielding his bow, had sworn before all the kings, “I shall not again enter Kuṇḍina if I do not kill Kṛṣṇa in battle and bring Rukmiṇī back with me. I swear this to you.”

verses.sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा रथमारुह्य सारथिं प्राह सत्वर: ।चोदयाश्वान् यत: कृष्ण: तस्य मे संयुगं भवेत् ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity uktvā ratham āruhyasārathiṁ prāha satvaraḥcodayāśvān yataḥ kṛṣṇaḥtasya me saṁyugaṁ bhavet
verses.text: Having said this, he had mounted his chariot and told his charioteer, “Drive the horses quickly to where Kṛṣṇa is. He and I must fight.

verses.sanskrit: अद्याहं निशितैर्बाणैर्गोपालस्य सुदुर्मते: ।नेष्ये वीर्यमदं येन स्वसा मे प्रसभं हृता ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: adyāhaṁ niśitair bāṇairgopālasya su-durmateḥneṣye vīrya-madaṁ yenasvasā me prasabhaṁ hṛtā
verses.text: “This wicked-minded cowherd boy, infatuated with His prowess, has violently abducted my sister. But today I will remove His pride with my sharp arrows.”

verses.sanskrit: विकत्थमान: कुमतिरीश्वरस्याप्रमाणवित् ।रथेनैकेन गोविन्दं तिष्ठ तिष्ठेत्यथाह्वयत् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: vikatthamānaḥ kumatirīśvarasyāpramāṇa-vitrathenaikena govindaṁtiṣṭha tiṣṭhety athāhvayat
verses.text: Boasting thus, foolish Rukmī, ignorant of the true extent of the Supreme Lord’s power, approached Lord Govinda in his lone chariot and challenged Him, “Just stand and fight!”

verses.sanskrit: धनुर्विकृष्य सुद‍ृढं जघ्ने कृष्णं त्रिभि: शरै: ।आह चात्र क्षणं तिष्ठ यदूनां कुलपांसन ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhanur vikṛṣya su-dṛḍhaṁjaghne kṛṣṇaṁ tribhiḥ śaraiḥāha cātra kṣaṇaṁ tiṣṭhayadūnāṁ kula-pāṁsana
verses.text: Rukmī drew his bow with great strength and struck Lord Kṛṣṇa with three arrows. Then he said, “Stand here for a moment, O defiler of the Yadu dynasty!

verses.sanskrit: यत्र यासि स्वसारं मे मुषित्वा ध्वाङ्‍क्षवद्धवि: ।हरिष्येऽद्य मदं मन्द मायिन: कूटयोधिन: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yatra yāsi svasāraṁ memuṣitvā dhvāṅkṣa-vad dhaviḥhariṣye ’dya madaṁ mandamāyinaḥ kūṭa-yodhinaḥ
verses.text: “Wherever You go, carrying off my sister like a crow stealing sacrificial butter, I will follow. This very day I shall relieve You of Your false pride, You fool, You deceiver, You cheater in battle!

verses.sanskrit: यावन्न मे हतो बाणै: शयीथा मुञ्च दारीकाम् ।स्मयन् कृष्णो धनुश्छित्त्वा षड्‍‌भिर्विव्याध रुक्‍मिणम् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: yāvan na me hato bāṇaiḥśayīthā muñca dārīkāmsmayan kṛṣṇo dhanuś chittvāṣaḍbhir vivyādha rukmiṇam
verses.text: “Release the girl before You are struck dead by my arrows and made to lie down!” In response to this, Lord Kṛṣṇa smiled, and with six arrows He struck Rukmī and broke his bow.

verses.sanskrit: अष्टभिश्चतुरो वाहान् द्वाभ्यां सूतं ध्वजं त्रिभि: ।स चान्यद्धनुराधाय कृष्णं विव्याध पञ्चभि: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: aṣṭabhiś caturo vāhāndvābhyāṁ sūtaṁ dhvajaṁ tribhiḥsa cānyad dhanur ādhāyakṛṣṇaṁ vivyādha pañcabhiḥ
verses.text: The Lord struck Rukmī’s four horses with eight arrows, his chariot driver with two, and the chariot’s flag with three. Rukmī grabbed another bow and struck Lord Kṛṣṇa with five arrows.

verses.sanskrit: तैस्ताडित: शरौघैस्तु चिच्छेद धनुरच्युत: ।पुनरन्यदुपादत्त तदप्यच्छिनदव्यय: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tais tāditaḥ śaraughais tuciccheda dhanur acyutaḥpunar anyad upādattatad apy acchinad avyayaḥ
verses.text: Although hit by these many arrows, Lord Acyuta again broke Rukmī’s bow. Rukmī picked up yet another bow, but the infallible Lord broke that one to pieces as well.

verses.sanskrit: परिघं पट्टिशं शूलं चर्मासी शक्तितोमरौ ।यद् यदायुधमादत्त तत्सर्वं सोऽच्छिनद्धरि: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: parighaṁ paṭṭiśaṁ śūlaṁcarmāsī śakti-tomarauyad yad āyudham ādattatat sarvaṁ so ’cchinad dhariḥ
verses.text: Iron bludgeon, three-pointed spear, sword and shield, pike, javelin — whatever weapon Rukmī picked up, Lord Hari smashed it to bits.

verses.sanskrit: ततो रथादवप्लुत्य खड्‍गपाणिर्जिघांसया ।कृष्णमभ्यद्रवत् क्रुद्ध: पतङ्ग इव पावकम् ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: tato rathād avaplutyakhaḍga-pāṇir jighāṁsayākṛṣṇam abhyadravat kruddhaḥpataṅga iva pāvakam
verses.text: Then Rukmī leaped down from his chariot and, sword in hand, rushed furiously toward Kṛṣṇa to kill Him, like a bird flying into the wind.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य चापतत: खड्‍गं तिलशश्चर्म चेषुभि: ।छित्त्वासिमाददे तिग्मं रुक्‍मिणं हन्तुमुद्यत: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya cāpatataḥ khaḍgaṁtilaśaś carma ceṣubhiḥchittvāsim ādade tigmaṁrukmiṇaṁ hantum udyataḥ
verses.text: As Rukmī attacked Him, the Lord shot arrows that broke Rukmī’s sword and shield into small pieces. Kṛṣṇa then took up His own sharp sword and prepared to kill Rukmī.

verses.sanskrit: द‍ृष्ट्वा भ्रातृवधोद्योगं रुक्‍मिणी भयविह्वला ।पतित्वा पादयोर्भर्तुरुवाच करुणं सती ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvā bhrātṛ-vadhodyogaṁrukmiṇī bhaya-vihvalāpatitvā pādayor bharturuvāca karuṇaṁ satī
verses.text: Seeing Lord Kṛṣṇa ready to kill her brother, saintly Rukmiṇī was filled with alarm. She fell at her husband’s feet and piteously spoke as follows.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीरुक्‍मिण्युवाचयोगेश्वराप्रमेयात्मन् देवदेव जगत्पते ।हन्तुं नार्हसि कल्याण भ्रातरं मे महाभुज ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-rukmiṇy uvācayogeśvarāprameyātmandeva-deva jagat-patehantuṁ nārhasi kalyāṇabhrātaraṁ me mahā-bhuja
verses.text: Śrī Rukmiṇī said: O controller of all mystic power, immeasurable one, Lord of lords, master of the universe! O all auspicious and mighty-armed one, please do not kill my brother!

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचतया परित्रासविकम्पिताङ्गयाशुचावशुष्यन्मुखरुद्धकण्ठया ।कातर्यविस्रंसितहेममालयागृहीतपाद: करुणो न्यवर्तत ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācatayā paritrāsa-vikampitāṅgayāśucāvaśuṣyan-mukha-ruddha-kaṇṭhayākātarya-visraṁsita-hema-mālayāgṛhīta-pādaḥ karuṇo nyavartata
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Rukmiṇī’s utter fear caused her limbs to tremble and her mouth to dry up, while her throat choked up out of sorrow. And in her agitation her golden necklace scattered. She grasped Kṛṣṇa’s feet, and the Lord, feeling compassionate, desisted.

verses.sanskrit: चैलेन बद्ध्वा तमसाधुकारीणंसश्मश्रुकेशं प्रवपन् व्यरूपयत् ।तावन्ममर्दु: परसैन्यमद्भ‍ुतंयदुप्रवीरा नलिनीं यथा गजा: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: cailena baddhvā tam asādhu-kārīṇaṁsa-śmaśru-keśaṁ pravapan vyarūpayattāvan mamarduḥ para-sainyam adbhutaṁyadu-pravīrā nalinīṁ yathā gajāḥ
verses.text: Lord Kṛṣṇa tied up the evil-doer with a strip of cloth. He then proceeded to disfigure Rukmī by comically shaving him, leaving parts of his mustache and hair. By that time the Yadu heroes had crushed the extraordinary army of their opponents, just as elephants crush a lotus flower.

verses.sanskrit: कृष्णान्तिकमुपव्रज्य दद‍ृशुस्तत्र रुक्‍मिणम् ।तथाभूतं हतप्रायं द‍ृष्ट्वा सङ्कर्षणो विभु: ।विमुच्य बद्धं करुणो भगवान् कृष्णमब्रवीत् ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛṣṇāntikam upavrajyadadṛśus tatra rukmiṇamtathā-bhūtaṁ hata-prāyaṁdṛṣṭvā saṅkarṣaṇo vibhuḥvimucya baddhaṁ karuṇobhagavān kṛṣṇam abravīt
verses.text: As the Yadus approached Lord Kṛṣṇa, they saw Rukmī in this sorry condition, practically dying of shame. When the all-powerful Lord Balarāma saw Rukmī, He compassionately released him and spoke the following to Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: असाध्विदं त्वया कृष्ण कृतमस्मज्जुगुप्सितम् ।वपनं श्मश्रुकेशानां वैरूप्यं सुहृदो वध: ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: asādhv idaṁ tvayā kṛṣṇakṛtam asmaj-jugupsitamvapanaṁ śmaśru-keśānāṁvairūpyaṁ suhṛdo vadhaḥ
verses.text: [Lord Balarāma said:] My dear Kṛṣṇa, You have acted improperly! This deed will bring shame on Us, for to disfigure a close relative by shaving off his mustache and hair is as good as killing him.

verses.sanskrit: मैवास्मान् साध्व्यसूयेथा भ्रातुर्वैरूप्यचिन्तया ।सुखदु:खदो न चान्योऽस्ति यत: स्वकृतभुक् पुमान् ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: maivāsmān sādhvy asūyethābhrātur vairūpya-cintayāsukha-duḥkha-do na cānyo ’stiyataḥ sva-kṛta-bhuk pumān
verses.text: Saintly lady, please do not be displeased with Us out of anxiety for your brother’s disfigurement. No one but oneself is responsible for one’s joy and grief, for a man experiences the result of his own deeds.

verses.sanskrit: बन्धुर्वधार्हदोषोऽपि न बन्धोर्वधमर्हति ।त्याज्य: स्वेनैव दोषेण हत: किं हन्यते पुन: ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: bandhur vadhārha-doṣo ’pina bandhor vadham arhatityājyaḥ svenaiva doṣeṇahataḥ kiṁ hanyate punaḥ
verses.text: [Again addressing Kṛṣṇa, Balarāma said:] A relative should not be killed even if his wrongdoing warrants capital punishment. Rather, he should be thrown out of the family. Since he has already been killed by his own sin, why kill him again?

verses.sanskrit: क्षत्रियाणामयं धर्म: प्रजापतिविनिर्मित: ।भ्रातापि भ्रातरं हन्याद् येन घोरतमस्तत: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: kṣatriyāṇām ayaṁ dharmaḥprajāpati-vinirmitaḥbhrātāpi bhrātaraṁ hanyādyena ghoratamas tataḥ
verses.text: [Turning to Rukmiṇī, Balarāma continued:] The code of sacred duty for warriors established by Lord Brahmā enjoins that one may have to kill even his own brother. That is indeed a most dreadful law.

verses.sanskrit: राज्यस्य भूमेर्वित्तस्य स्‍त्रियो मानस्य तेजस: ।मानिनोऽन्यस्य वा हेतो: श्रीमदान्धा: क्षिपन्ति हि ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: rājyasya bhūmer vittasyastriyo mānasya tejasaḥmānino ’nyasya vā hetoḥśrī-madāndhāḥ kṣipanti hi
verses.text: [Again Balarāma addressed Kṛṣṇa:] Blinded by conceit with their personal opulences, proud men offend others for the sake of such things as kingdom, land, wealth, women, honor and power.

verses.sanskrit: तवेयं विषमा बुद्धि: सर्वभूतेषु दुर्हृदाम् ।यन्मन्यसे सदाभद्रं सुहृदां भद्रमज्ञवत् ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: taveyaṁ viṣamā buddhiḥsarva-bhūteṣu durhṛdāmyan manyase sadābhadraṁsuhṛdāṁ bhadram ajña-vat
verses.text: [To Rukmiṇī Balarāma said:] Your attitude is unfair, for like an ignorant person you wish good to those who are inimical to all living beings and who have done evil to your true well-wishers.

verses.sanskrit: आत्ममोहो नृणामेव कल्पते देवमायया ।सुहृद् दुर्हृदुदासीन इति देहात्ममानिनाम् ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: ātma-moho nṛṇām evakalpate deva-māyayāsuhṛd durhṛd udāsīnaiti dehātma-māninām
verses.text: The Supreme Lord’s Māyā makes men forget their real selves, and thus, taking the body for the self, they consider others to be friends, enemies or neutral parties.

verses.sanskrit: एक एव परो ह्यात्मा सर्वेषामपि देहिनाम् ।नानेव गृह्यते मूढैर्यथा ज्योतिर्यथा नभ: ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: eka eva paro hy ātmāsarveṣām api dehināmnāneva gṛhyate mūḍhairyathā jyotir yathā nabhaḥ
verses.text: Those who are bewildered perceive the one Supreme Soul, who resides in all embodied beings, as many, just as one may perceive the light in the sky, or the sky itself, as many.

verses.sanskrit: देह आद्यन्तवानेष द्रव्यप्राणगुणात्मक: ।आत्मन्यविद्यया क्लृप्त: संसारयति देहिनम् ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: deha ādy-antavān eṣadravya-prāṇa-guṇātmakaḥātmany avidyayā kḷptaḥsaṁsārayati dehinam
verses.text: This material body, which has a beginning and an end, is composed of the physical elements, the senses and the modes of nature. The body, imposed on the self by material ignorance, causes one to experience the cycle of birth and death.

verses.sanskrit: नात्मनोऽन्येन संयोगो वियोगश्चासत: सति ।तद्धेतुत्वात्तत्प्रसिद्धेर्द‍ृग्‌रूपाभ्यां यथा रवे: ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: nātmano ’nyena saṁyogoviyogaś casataḥ satitad-dhetutvāt tat-prasiddherdṛg-rūpābhyāṁ yathā raveḥ
verses.text: O intelligent lady, the soul never undergoes contact with or separation from insubstantial, material objects, because the soul is their very origin and illuminator. Thus the soul resembles the sun, which neither comes in contact with nor separates from the sense of sight and what is seen.

verses.sanskrit: जन्मादयस्तु देहस्य विक्रियानात्मन: क्व‍‍चित् ।कलानामिव नैवेन्दोर्मृतिर्ह्यस्य कुहूरिव ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: janmādayas tu dehasyavikriyā nātmanaḥ kvacitkalānām iva naivendormṛtir hy asya kuhūr iva
verses.text: Birth and other transformations are undergone by the body but never by the self, just as change occurs for the moon’s phases but never for the moon, though the new-moon day may be called the moon’s “death.”

verses.sanskrit: यथा शयान आत्मानं विषयान् फलमेव च ।अनुभुङ्क्ते ऽप्यसत्यर्थे तथाप्नोत्यबुधो भवम् ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: yathā śayāna ātmānaṁviṣayān phalam eva caanubhuṅkte ’py asaty arthetathāpnoty abudho bhavam
verses.text: As a sleeping person perceives himself, the objects of sense enjoyment and the fruits of his acts within the illusion of a dream, so one who is unintelligent undergoes material existence.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मादज्ञानजं शोकमात्मशोषविमोहनम् ।तत्त्वज्ञानेन निर्हृत्य स्वस्था भव शुचिस्मिते ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmād ajñāna-jaṁ śokamātma-śoṣa-vimohanamtattva-jñānena nirhṛtyasva-sthā bhava śuci-smite
verses.text: Therefore, with transcendental knowledge dispel the grief that is weakening and confounding your mind. Please resume your natural mood, O princess of the pristine smile.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचएवं भगवता तन्वी रामेण प्रतिबोधिता ।वैमनस्यं परित्यज्य मनो बुद्ध्या समादधे ॥ ५० ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaevaṁ bhagavatā tanvīrāmeṇa pratibodhitāvaimanasyaṁ parityajyamano buddhyā samādadhe
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Thus enlightened by Lord Balarāma, slender Rukmiṇī forgot her depression and steadied her mind by spiritual intelligence.

verses.sanskrit: प्राणावशेष उत्सृष्टो द्विड्‍‌भिर्हतबलप्रभ: ।स्मरन् विरूपकरणं वितथात्ममनोरथ: ।चक्रे भोजकटं नाम निवासाय महत्पुरम् ॥ ५१ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāṇāvaśeṣa utsṛṣṭodviḍbhir hata-bala-prabhaḥsmaran virūpa-karaṇaṁvitathātma-manorathaḥcakre bhojakaṭaṁ nāmanivāsāya mahat puram
verses.text: Left with only his life air, cast out by his enemies and deprived of his strength and bodily radiance, Rukmī could not forget how he had been disfigured. In frustration he constructed for his residence a large city, which he called Bhojakaṭa.

verses.sanskrit: अहत्वा दुर्मतिं कृष्णमप्रत्यूह्य यवीयसीम् ।कुण्डिनं न प्रवेक्ष्यामीत्युक्त्वा तत्रावसद् रुषा ॥ ५२ ॥
verses.transliteration: ahatvā durmatiṁ kṛṣṇamapratyūhya yavīyasīmkuṇḍinaṁ na pravekṣyāmītyuktvā tatrāvasad ruṣā
verses.text: Because he had promised “I will not reenter Kuṇḍina until I have killed wicked Kṛṣṇa and brought back my younger sister,” in a mood of angry frustration Rukmī took up residence at that very place.

verses.sanskrit: भगवान् भीष्मकसुतामेवं निर्जित्य भूमिपान् ।पुरमानीय विधिवदुपयेमे कुरूद्वह ॥ ५३ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhagavān bhīṣmaka-sutāmevaṁ nirjitya bhūmi-pānpuram ānīya vidhi-vadupayeme kurūdvaha
verses.text: Thus defeating all the opposing kings, the Supreme Personality of Godhead brought the daughter of Bhīṣmaka to His capital and married her according to the Vedic injunctions, O protector of the Kurus.

verses.sanskrit: तदा महोत्सवो नृणां यदुपुर्यां गृहे गृहे ।अभूदनन्यभावानां कृष्णे यदुपतौ नृप ॥ ५४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tadā mahotsavo nṝṇāṁyadu-puryāṁ gṛhe gṛheabhūd ananya-bhāvānāṁkṛṣṇe yadu-patau nṛpa
verses.text: At that time, O King, there was great rejoicing in all the homes of Yadupurī, whose citizens loved only Kṛṣṇa, chief of the Yadus.

verses.sanskrit: नरा नार्यश्च मुदिता: प्रमृष्टमणिकुण्डला: ।पारिबर्हमुपाजह्रुर्वरयोश्चित्रवाससो: ॥ ५५ ॥
verses.transliteration: narā nāryaś ca muditāḥpramṛṣṭa-maṇi-kuṇḍalāḥpāribarham upājahrurvarayoś citra-vāsasoḥ
verses.text: All the men and women, full of joy and adorned with shining jewels and earrings, brought wedding presents, which they reverently offered to the exquisitely dressed groom and bride.

verses.sanskrit: सा वृष्णिपुर्युत्तम्भितेन्द्रकेतुभि-र्विचित्रमाल्याम्बररत्नतोरणै: ।बभौ प्रतिद्वार्युपक्लृप्तमङ्गलै-रापूर्णकुम्भागुरुधूपदीपकै: ॥ ५६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sā vṛṣṇi-pury uttambhitendra-ketubhirvicitra-mālyāmbara-ratna-toraṇaiḥbabhau prati-dvāry upakḷpta-maṅgalairāpūrṇa-kumbhāguru-dhūpa-dīpakaiḥ
verses.text: The city of the Vṛṣṇis appeared most beautiful: there were tall, festive columns, and also archways decorated with flower garlands, cloth banners and precious gems. Arrangements of auspicious, full waterpots, aguru-scented incense, and lamps graced every doorway.

verses.sanskrit: सिक्तमार्गा मदच्युद्भ‍िराहूतप्रेष्ठभूभुजाम् ।गजैर्द्वा:सु परामृष्टरम्भापूगोपशोभिता ॥ ५७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sikta-mārgā mada-cyudbhirāhūta-preṣṭha-bhūbhujāmgajair dvāḥsu parāmṛṣṭa-rambhā-pūgopaśobhitā
verses.text: The city’s streets were cleansed by the intoxicated elephants belonging to the beloved kings who were guests at the wedding, and these elephants further enhanced the beauty of the city by placing trunks of plantain and betel-nut trees in all the doorways.

verses.sanskrit: कुरुसृञ्जयकैकेयविदर्भयदुकुन्तय: ।मिथो मुमुदिरे तस्मिन् सम्भ्रमात् परिधावताम् ॥ ५८ ॥
verses.transliteration: kuru-sṛñjaya-kaikeya-vidarbha-yadu-kuntayaḥmitho mumudire tasminsambhramāt paridhāvatām
verses.text: Those who belonged to the royal families of the Kuru, Sṛñjaya, Kaikeya, Vidarbha, Yadu and Kunti clans joyfully met one another in the midst of the crowds of people excitedly running here and there.

verses.sanskrit: रुक्‍मिण्या हरणं श्रुत्वा गीयमानं ततस्तत: ।राजानो राजकन्याश्च बभूवुर्भृशविस्मिता: ॥ ५९ ॥
verses.transliteration: rukmiṇyā haraṇaṁ śrutvāgīyamānaṁ tatas tataḥrājāno rāja-kanyāś cababhūvur bhṛśa-vismitāḥ
verses.text: The kings and their daughters were totally astonished to hear the story of Rukmiṇī’s abduction, which was being glorified in song everywhere.

verses.sanskrit: द्वारकायामभूद् राजन् महामोद: पुरौकसाम् ।रुक्‍मिण्या रमयोपेतं द‍ृष्ट्वा कृष्णं श्रिय: पतिम् ॥ ६० ॥
verses.transliteration: dvārakāyām abhūd rājanmahā-modaḥ puraukasāmrukmiṇyā ramayopetaṁdṛṣṭvā kṛṣṇaṁ śriyaḥ patim
verses.text: Dvārakā’s citizens were overjoyed to see Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of all opulence, united with Rukmiṇī, the goddess of fortune.
